contaminant.name: Zinc Electroplating
contaminant.description: Zinc-plating contamination appears as thin protective layer on steel and iron surfaces, so it forms during corrosion-resistant coating processes to prevent rust. This layer shows unique even distribution and metallic sheen that differs from rust or scale buildup, and it adheres tightly on steel while flaking more easily on iron during exposure. Removal challenges include risk of base material damage because of strong bonding, but laser cleaning works effectively so it ablates the layer precisely without harming underlying steel or iron.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Zinc Oxide generation detected - low toxicity risk
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
